About: It is about a noble family of German origin.
Affects: It affects the Duchy of Swabia from 1079.
It affects the Holy Roman Empire from 1138 until 1254.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Duchy of Swabia.
It is associated with the Holy Roman Empire.
It is associated with Italy.
It is associated with Burgundy.
It is associated with the Staufen Castle.
It is associated with Hohenstaufen mountain.
It is associated with the Swabian Jura.
It is associated with Göppingen.
It is associated with the Salian dynasty.
It is associated with the House of Welf.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Holy Roman Empire.
BuiltBy: It was built by Duke Frederick I of Swabia.
BuiltIn: It was built in the latter half of the 11th century.
CausedBy: It is caused by the succession controversy after the death of Emperor Henry V.
HasA: It has prominent rulers like Frederick I, Henry VI, and Frederick II.
It has a castle on Hohenstaufen mountain.
It has a connection to the town of Waiblingen.
It has a pro-imperial Ghibelline faction in Italy.
It has a lineage traced back to counts of Riesgau.
It has a progenitor Frederick (d. about 1075).
It has a son Frederick of Büren (c. 1020–1053).
It has a son Frederick I appointed Duke of Swabia.
It has a brother Otto elevated to the Strasbourg bishopric.
It has a son Duke Frederick II succeeding in 1105.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by ruling the Duchy of Swabia.
It is characterized by ruling the Holy Roman Empire.
It is characterized by reaching the Empire's greatest territorial extent.
It is characterized by its prominent rulers ascending the imperial throne.
It is characterized by its name derived from Hohenstaufen Castle.
It is characterized by its name being preferred as 'Staufer' in German historiography.
It is characterized by its name deriving from 'stouf' meaning 'chalice'.
It is characterized by members occasionally using the toponymic surname de Stauf.
It is characterized by its connection to the Waiblingen name.
It is characterized by its Italian historiography name Svevi.
Influenced: It influenced the Ghibelline faction of the Italian civic rivalries.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Salian dynasty.
It was influenced by the House of Welf.
It was influenced by Pope Alexander III.
It was influenced by Bernard of Clairvaux.
KnownAs: It is known as the Staufer.
It is known as the Svevi in Italian historiography.
